threads.topic: Electrical requirements for connectors in engine management systems
threads.summary: The thread discusses the electrical requirements for connectors used with Bosch ECU (ETB) in an engine management system. It is recommended to have at least 1 via per amp, and since a Bosch ETB has a nominal current draw of 5-8 amps, it should have at least 5 vias to ensure durability under daily use conditions.
key_details: At least 1 via per amp
Bosch ECU (ETB) nominal current draw: 5-8 amps
Recommended minimum number of vias for a Bosch ETB: 5
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Electrical requirements for connectors in engine management systems
CONTRIBUTORS: @jeybee
SUMMARY: The thread discusses the electrical requirements for connectors used with Bosch ECU (ETB) in an engine management system. It is recommended to have at least 1 via per amp, and since a Bosch ETB has a nominal current draw of 5-8 amps, it should have at least 5 vias to ensure durability under daily use conditions.
KEY_DETAILS:
- At least 1 via per amp
- Bosch ECU (ETB) nominal current draw: 5-8 amps
- Recommended minimum number of vias for a Bosch ETB: 5

threads.topic: Reducing production costs through single-sided PCB design
threads.summary: @jeybee suggested reducing production costs by designing a board with components only on one side. In response, @GGGI clarified that both sides are acceptable as long as the number of components on the second side is minimal and allows for easy hand soldering.
key_details: Components should primarily be placed on one side of the PCB
Both sides can be used but must have a minimal number of components
The design needs to allow for easy hand soldering

KEY_DETAILS:
- Components should primarily be placed on one side of the PCB
- Both sides can be used but must have a minimal number of components
- The design needs to allow for easy hand soldering

threads.topic: SD Card Requirements for rusEFI/epicEFI
threads.summary: User @jeybee inquired about future plans regarding SD card requirements for rusEFI/epicEFI. The key response from @admin_user was that the project aims to maintain compatibility with a wide range of SD cards but recommends using high-capacity (at least 16GB) and fast read/write speed (Class 10 or UHS-1 rated) SD cards for optimal performance.
key_details: Recommended minimum SD card capacity: 16GB
Recommended SD card speed rating: Class 10 or UHS-1
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: SD Card Requirements for rusEFI/epicEFI
CONTRIBUTORS: @jeybee, @admin_user
SUMMARY: User @jeybee inquired about future plans regarding SD card requirements for rusEFI/epicEFI. The key response from @admin_user was that the project aims to maintain compatibility with a wide range of SD cards but recommends using high-capacity (at least 16GB) and fast read/write speed (Class 10 or UHS-1 rated) SD cards for optimal performance.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Recommended minimum SD card capacity: 16GB
- Recommended SD card speed rating: Class 10 or UHS-1

threads.topic: SD card removal and engine safety
threads.summary: User @jeybee inquired about the consequences of removing an SD card from a rusEFI/epicEFI system. Developer @epicEFIdev clarified that removing or losing contact with the SD card will not cause the engine to shut down, ensuring safe operation.
key_details: Removing or losing contact with the SD card does not result in engine shutdown
The system is designed to maintain safe engine operation even if the SD card is removed

threads.topic: Preference between .hex and .bin files for engine management

Both .hex and .bin formats are supported

- Both .hex and .bin formats are supported

threads.topic: EGT Tuning Limitations for Fuel Efficiency
threads.summary: The thread discusses the limitations of tuning based on exhaust gas temperature (EGT) in achieving optimal fuel efficiency. Specifically, it mentions that EGT readings form a V or mountain-shaped curve when plotted against air-fuel ratio (AFR), making direct tuning off EGT ineffective.
key_details: EGT forms a "V" or "mountain-shaped" curve with AFR
Direct EGT-based tuning for fuel efficiency is not recommended
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: EGT Tuning Limitations for Fuel Efficiency
CONTRIBUTORS: @Joesphan
SUMMARY: The thread discusses the limitations of tuning based on exhaust gas temperature (EGT) in achieving optimal fuel efficiency. Specifically, it mentions that EGT readings form a V or mountain-shaped curve when plotted against air-fuel ratio (AFR), making direct tuning off EGT ineffective.
KEY_DETAILS:
- EGT forms a "V" or "mountain-shaped" curve with AFR
- Direct EGT-based tuning for fuel efficiency is not recommended

Jeybee provides feedback, noting that the timing looks cleaner but suggests that the VE setting might be too rich at higher pressures.

Timing is aggressive up to 4500 RPM
Air-fuel ratio (A/F) is already at 9:1 at 12 psi

threads.topic: Issues with nitrous staging and injector output behavior at certain percentages
threads.summary: @tmbryhn reported issues with nitrous staging and injector output behavior when transitioning from 99% to 100%. At 99%, the outputs function correctly, but at 100%, the outputs become erratic despite correct pulse width (PW) commands. Additionally, secondary injectors need a minimum of 10% staging to activate properly.
key_details: Nitrous uses TPS actual in ETB mode
Batch fuel when cam not present
Erratic injector output behavior at 100% staging
Secondary injectors require above 10% staging to activate
Quick-fix: Define max 99% and min 10% in .ini file
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Issues with nitrous staging and injector output behavior at certain percentages
CONTRIBUTORS: @tmbryhn
SUMMARY: @tmbryhn reported issues with nitrous staging and injector output behavior when transitioning from 99% to 100%. At 99%, the outputs function correctly, but at 100%, the outputs become erratic despite correct pulse width (PW) commands. Additionally, secondary injectors need a minimum of 10% staging to activate properly.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Nitrous uses TPS actual in ETB mode
- Batch fuel when cam not present
- Erratic injector output behavior at 100% staging
- Secondary injectors require above 10% staging to activate
- Quick-fix: Define max 99% and min 10% in .ini file

threads.topic: Unassigning and reassigning MAP pins for DFCO settings
threads.summary: @MykkH discussed the issue of unassigning the Manifold Absolute Pressure (MAP) pin, which would render MAP settings in Demand-Fuel-Cut-Off (DFCO) ineffective. As an alternative solution, they suggested using a feature that allows multiple inputs on the same pin to assign both MAP and Throttle Position Sensor (TPS) signals to the same pin, creating a faux-MAP input.
key_details: Unassigning the MAP pin would make DFCO settings inactive
Suggested allowing multiple inputs on the same pin for MAP and TPS
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Unassigning and reassigning MAP pins for DFCO settings
CONTRIBUTORS: @MykkH

As an alternative solution, they suggested using a feature that allows multiple inputs on the same pin to assign both MAP and Throttle Position Sensor (TPS) signals to the same pin, creating a faux-MAP input.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Unassigning the MAP pin would make DFCO settings inactive
- Suggested allowing multiple inputs on the same pin for MAP and TPS

threads.topic: Controlling Solenoids in Engine Management Systems
threads.summary: In response to a question about controlling solenoids, @Robb235 explained that they use high-side driving for their solenoids. A transmission signal conditioner board is used to convert the 5V signals from the microcontroller (mega) into 12V signals required by the solenoids using MOSFETs.
key_details: High-side driving method
Transmission signal conditioner board converts 5V signals to 12V for solenoid control
Use of MOSFETs in the transmission signal conditioner board
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Controlling Solenoids in Engine Management Systems
CONTRIBUTORS: @SanGawku, @Robb235
SUMMARY: In response to a question about controlling solenoids, @Robb235 explained that they use high-side driving for their solenoids. A transmission signal conditioner board is used to convert the 5V signals from the microcontroller (mega) into 12V signals required by the solenoids using MOSFETs.
KEY_DETAILS:
- High-side driving method
- Transmission signal conditioner board converts 5V signals to 12V for solenoid control
- Use of MOSFETs in the transmission signal conditioner board

Resistor divider used to reduce voltage to ~5V for input into rusEFI/epicEFI

threads.topic: Knock detection functionality in epicEFI
threads.summary: User @ggurov asked if the knock detection feature was functional in epicEFI and requested a bench test to verify its operation. The response from @epicdev confirmed that the knock detection is operational but requires proper configuration of thresholds for accurate detection.
key_details: Bench testing required to confirm functionality
Proper configuration of knock threshold settings needed
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Knock detection functionality in epicEFI
CONTRIBUTORS: @ggurov, @epicdev
SUMMARY: User @ggurov asked if the knock detection feature was functional in epicEFI and requested a bench test to verify its operation. The response from @epicdev confirmed that the knock detection is operational but requires proper configuration of thresholds for accurate detection.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Bench testing required to confirm functionality
- Proper configuration of knock threshold settings needed
